incident_report:
# Incident Report: Dungeon Agent Run (Seed 77)

**Summary:** Both Agent A and Agent B failed to complete the dungeon objective within the 50-turn limit, reaching turn 51 with zero progress. The run was severely hampered by repeated spurious_block anomalies that prevented normal movement, occurring 18 times across both agents.

**Root Cause:** The agents encountered an unusually high frequency of movement-blocking anomalies starting early in the run (Turn 7 for Agent A). These spurious blocks appeared randomly throughout the attempt, with Agent B particularly affected in the mid-to-late game (Turns 24-49). Neither agent acquired a key pickup or unlocked any doors, suggesting they were unable to advance past initial obstacles.

**Critical Decision Points:** The most notable failure occurred around Turn 7-20, where Agent A encountered 5 anomalies in just 14 turns, effectively preventing sustained progress. Agent B's performance degraded significantly from Turn 24 onward, hitting anomalies in 7 of the final 26 turns. The lack of any DM interactions or inter-agent communication (0 messages sent) suggests the agents did not attempt to coordinate strategy or seek guidance when movement became impossible.

**Conclusion:** This run appears to have been compromised by environmental RNG rather than agent logic failure—the sheer volume
